sections.heading: What this tracker does
sections.text:
The Portfolio Tracker is a single self-contained HTML file that runs entirely in your browser — no server, no installation, no account required. It solves the core challenge of managing a complex digital asset portfolio across multiple wallets, liquidity pools, staking vaults, and speculative positions, all in one place with live prices.

Your data never leaves your machine. Everything is stored in your browser's local storage and a linked .json backup file on your own drive.

sections.heading: The seven tabs — what each does
sections.text:
📊 Summary — Portfolio command center. Total value, deployed capital, P&L across all areas, and the IL Break-Even Calculator for modeling new positions before committing capital.

🔁 LP Pools — Tracks concentrated liquidity positions (Uniswap v3, Aerodrome, Orca, Beefy vaults). Live pricing, impermanent loss calculation, yield logging, and the Beat HODL? scorecard.

🏦 Vaults — Single-asset yield positions: staking, lending, LSTs. Tracks APY, yield earned, and net result vs entry value.

💼 Holdings — Spot tokens across all wallets and chains. Cost basis, P&L, allocation %, and per-holding price alerts (buy trigger, stop loss, T1/T2/T3).

⚡ Hedge — Calculates optimal perpetual short positions on Hyperliquid to protect BTC, ETH, and SOL exposure. Live IV from Deribit, funding rates from Hyperliquid, and a 5-step formula produce a regime-based hedge ratio. Performance chart shows hedge effectiveness over time.

📁 History — Archives all closed pools, rebalances, yield logs, and transactions. Full editing capability on historical entries. Tax-ready CSV export.

💎 GEMs — Watchlist for speculative tokens. Buy triggers, stop losses, three take-profit tiers, Vol/MC momentum signals, and sparklines.

sections.heading: Key concepts to understand
sections.text:
Beat HODL? — The fundamental LP question: has the yield earned by providing liquidity outweighed the impermanent loss vs simply holding the tokens? Shown as ✓ YES / ✗ NO on every pool.

Hedge ratio — The percentage of your crypto exposure protected by a short position. Hard capped at 60% — you always retain meaningful upside exposure. The formula uses implied volatility, 7-day trend, and funding rate to recommend the optimal ratio automatically.

Net carry — Pool yield earned minus annual funding cost on the hedge. Positive carry means the hedge is net profitable.

Funded From Holdings — When adding capital to a pool or vault, this feature auto-deducts the source tokens from your Holdings and logs Transfer transactions — keeping all balances correct without manual editing.

Backup envelope — Your .json backup file contains everything: portfolio data, hedge chart history, activation token, Pushover keys, and settings. Linking the backup file on any machine restores the complete tracker instantly.

sections.heading: Getting started — four steps
sections.text:
1. Open in Chrome or Edge — double-click the HTML file. Firefox is not recommended (File System API limitations).

2. Link your backup file — click ⛁ Data ▾ → Link / Change File → select or create a .json file in the same folder. Auto-save activates immediately (every 7 minutes, silently).

3. Add your first position — go to the tab that matches what you want to track. Click the + button. For LP Pools: enter both token symbols, current quantities, entry value, and your price range.

4. Refresh prices — click ↻ Refresh in the header. Prices update from CoinGecko and the live header bar populates. From here, everything is live.

sections.heading: Data protection
sections.text:
Auto-save — every 7 minutes when a backup file is linked. Also saves when you switch away from the browser tab.

Manual save — ⛁ Data ▾ → Save / Export Backup downloads a dated .json backup to your drive.

After a cache clear — if your browser cache is cleared, localStorage is wiped. Re-open the tracker and link your .json backup file — all data restores immediately, including hedge chart history.

Moving to a new machine — copy the entire tracker folder (HTML file + backup .json + help_img/ folder). Open the HTML in Chrome and link the backup file. Activation, settings, and all data restore automatically.

sections.heading: Common first-time questions
sections.text:
Why isn't a token price loading? CoinGecko may be rate-limited — wait 60 seconds and click ↻ Refresh. If a specific token always shows — , its CoinGecko ID may be wrong — edit the entry and search for the correct ID.

How do I activate the tracker? Your Install ID is shown on the activation screen. Send it to the author and an activation code will be returned. The code is stored in your backup file and restores automatically on any future import.

What is the Hedge tab for? If you hold BTC, ETH, or SOL in pools or vaults, the Hedge tab helps you size a protective short position on Hyperliquid that offsets downside risk while keeping your upside exposure. It is entirely optional — all other tabs function independently.

Is my data safe? Yes — no data ever leaves your machine. The only external calls are to CoinGecko (prices), Deribit (implied volatility), Hyperliquid (funding rates), and a Cloudflare Worker (help content verification only). No portfolio data is transmitted in any of these calls.

sections.heading: What this tab does
sections.text:
The Summary Tab provides a bird's-eye view of your entire portfolio — Total Value, Deployed Capital, overall P&L, and a breakdown by area (Pools, Vaults, Holdings, GEMs). [img:1]
The Toolbar is on the top of every screen.  This controls many aspects of the application:
[img:2]

sections.heading: How it functions
sections.text: All values are calculated in real time from live prices using a CoinGecko API (program interface). A IL (Impermanent Loss) Break-Even Calculator on the lower half of the Tab, allows you to model impermanent loss scenarios for any pool type before committing capital. It estimates how long it will take for Pool Yield to overcome a specific Pool's IL. This is a prospective means to estimate a Pool's profitability before entering.

sections.heading: How to enter data
sections.text: The Summary Tab has no data entry — this tab summarizes data from all other tabs. Set your Inception Date by clicking the date field to track portfolio-wide performance from a specific start date.

sections.heading: Further actions
sections.text: Use the IL Calculator to model new positions before adding them. Click ↻ Refresh in the header to update all live prices. Here is a modeled SOL/USDC pool, estimating the future price movement. [img:4] We input the numbers into the IL Calculator and see this result: [img:5] In this example: If the pool remains in range, the profit offsets any impermanent loss in the first day: [img:6]

sections.heading: How to interpret
sections.text:
Deployed = what you put in. Current = what it's worth now. Overall P&L = current minus deployed. Each Tab reveals its own Deployed $ vs Current$ vs P&L.
The IL Calculator helps estimate how long it will take for Yield to be greater than Impermanent loss when evaluating a Pool before entry.  See "further action" here.

sections.heading: Common questions
sections.text:
Why does P&L show — ? Prices haven't loaded yet — click Refresh.
Why doesn't it match my pool totals exactly? Pool P&L includes yield; Holdings P&L is price appreciation only.

sections.heading: What this tab does
sections.text:
The Liquidity Pool Tab manages all active liquidity pool positions — Token quantities are tracked, live values, yield harvested, range high and low, IL vs HODL comparison, and annualized returns.  Customized alerts notify you when range limits are near.  These are on-screen and can also be sent as phone alerts. [img:1]
Phone alerts appear along with audible notification:  [img:2]

sections.heading: How it functions
sections.text: Live prices originate from a CoinGecko API. The ratio between Token A and Token B is compared to your set range (Low/High) to determine range status. IL is calculated in real time, using the pool model appropriate for the Pool Platform (v2, v3, Curve, Balancer).

sections.heading: How to enter data
sections.text: Click + Add Pool. Enter both token symbols, current quantities, entry value, and your price range. Use ✎ to edit any row. Click ▶ on a row to update token quantities and log yield harvest. It is important to realize there is no necessity to update pools frequently. The accuracy will improve with update and harvest, but whenever performed you are up-to-date. To Add a new Pool: Select "Add Pool" on the top right of the Liquidity Pool List. Fill in the appropriate data. [img:4] Entry Token number = Current number at Pool deposit. Enter both. The Platform Name dictates the Pool Math utilized for accurate calculation. [img:8]

sections.heading: Further actions
sections.text:
Log yield harvests regularly using the ▶ expand panel. [img:5] 
When rebalancing use ✎ → Rebalance to reset your entry point. [img:6]
At that point, the Pool Values move to Closed Pool History.  "Rebalance" leaves the Pool in place. This then requires an update to the new Pool $ Value and Token A, and Token B quantities.   Use "Close" when exiting a Pool entirely.  Again, the Pool History is stored in Closed Pool History.

sections.heading: How to interpret
sections.text:
Range badge: In Range · Watch (within 20% of boundary) · Act Now (within 10%) · OUT OF RANGE.
Net/Ann%: Total return including yield, annualized.
Beat HODL? Pool+Yield vs simply holding the tokens. [img:7]

sections.heading: Common questions
sections.text:
Why is Live Ratio showing — ? A token price failed to load — try Refresh.
What pool model should I use? Uniswap v3/Orca = v3, Uniswap v2/Sushi = v2, Curve/Beefy stable pairs = Curve, Balancer = Balancer. The Pool Mathematical model should be recognized by the Pool Platform input on Deposit using the Portfolio Tracker Deposit input.

sections.heading: What this tab does
sections.text: The Vault tab tracks single-asset yield positions — staking, lending vaults, LSTs, and any strategy where you deposit one token and earn yield on it over time. [img:1]

sections.heading: How it functions
sections.text: Current value = Qty × Live Price. If you enter an APY, it estimates yield earned based on days since entry. You can override this with actual yield logged intermittently as desired.

sections.heading: How to enter data
sections.text: Click + Add Vault. Enter the token, platform, quantity, APY (optional), entry date, and entry value. Update Qty when your staked amount changes. Enter actual Yield Earned $ when you harvest. To Add a New Vault, Select +Add Vault in the right upper corner of the Vault List, fill in the appropriate information and Save the Vault. [img:2]

sections.heading: Further actions
sections.text: Update yield earned periodically. If you add more capital, edit the vault and adjust qty and entry value. Use Close via the Edit modal to archive to Vault History when exiting.

sections.heading: How to interpret
sections.text:
Ann. Return: Combines price appreciation + yield, annualized. EST means it's using your APY estimate rather than logged yield.
Yield APR = pure yield return excluding price movement.

sections.heading: Common questions
sections.text:
Why is Current showing — ? Token price not loaded — check the CoinGecko ID is correct.
My vault shows a loss — why? The token price may have fallen since entry. Entry value reflects what you deposited, not what it's worth now.

sections.heading: What this tab does
sections.text: Holdings or Assets tracks all spot holdings — This includes tokens held in wallets, exchanges, or hardware devices. This maintains the live price of all assets, the portfolio current total value of each asset, and Profit or Loss relative to your cost basis. The 24h% change, average cost basis, current value, allocation %, and unrealized P&L. [img:1]

sections.heading: How it functions
sections.text: In the Holdings Tab, each row is one token/wallet/chain combination. Average cost updates automatically using weighted average when you buy more. The Group button clusters holdings by wallet for a cleaner view. By changing the hierarchy (center top), you can view each asset across all Wallets and Exchanges, or view by Wallet showing the content of each.  This can be broken down further by Chain to view as an example, Ethereum on each chain on a specific wallet.  This helps you to understand what is available for gas fees and what can be used by assembling the same asset but from different chains.

sections.heading: How to enter data
sections.text: Click + Add Holding or use Buy/Sell/Swap/Transfer actions on an existing row. For buys, enter qty and price — cost basis is calculated and maintained automatically. This is also the case across the application Tabs. For stablecoins, the price is locked at $1. [img:2]

sections.heading: Further actions
sections.text: Use the 🔔 alert button on any row to set price alerts (buy trigger, stop loss, and Take Profit level: 1,2, or 3. Use Swap to record token-to-token trades. Use Transfer to move between wallets or from Central Exchange to your Wallet or your Wallet to Central exchange, while maintaining cost basis.[img:4]

sections.heading: How to interpret
sections.text: Avg Cost: Your blended entry price across all purchases. Alloc %: Each holding as a percentage of total spot portfolio value. P&L $ is your unrealized Gain or Loss relative to the cost basis of that asset in your Portfolio.

sections.heading: Common questions
sections.text: Why does a token show "—" for price? The CoinGecko ID may be wrong — re-search for the correct token, being sure to select an option below the search which shows the available matches. How do I record a sale?  Use the Sell action on the holding row — it reduces qty and logs the transaction.

sections.heading: What this tab does
sections.text: Calculates optimal short hedge sizes for BTC, ETH, and SOL to protect your LP and vault positions from downside price risk. Tracks open shorts on Hyperliquid and monitors funding rates.

sections.heading: How it functions
sections.text: The optimal ratio is calculated from IV (implied volatility), 7-day trend, funding rate, your pool yield APY, and BTC/asset stability signals. Funding rates and IV fetch live from Hyperliquid and Deribit.

sections.heading: How to enter data
sections.text: Use the slider on each asset card to set your hedge ratio. Click + Open Position to log a short — enter asset, margin ($), leverage, and entry price. Log HL deposits/withdrawals via the HL Deposit/Withdraw button.

sections.heading: Further actions
sections.text: Click ↻ Refresh to fetch latest funding rates and IV. Close positions via the Close button on Open Positions. Check the calculation detail (▶ Show) on each card to understand how the ratio was derived.

sections.heading: How to interpret
sections.text:
Funding Rate: Negative = shorts paying longs (costs you). Positive = longs paying shorts (you earn).
Rate / Funding column: Current 8hr rate · cumulative net earned or paid.
Net carry: Annual pool yield minus annual funding cost — positive means hedging is net profitable.

sections.heading: Common questions
sections.text:
Why is funding rate showing — ? Click Refresh — Hyperliquid data fetches on demand.
What does negative net carry mean? Funding cost exceeds pool yield — consider reducing hedge ratio.
What is the Limit Buy checkbox? Signals recovery conviction and reduces the recommended hedge by ~18%.

sections.heading: What this tab does
sections.text: Archives all closed and rebalanced pool positions, yield harvest logs, and transactions. Your permanent record for performance analysis and tax reporting.

sections.heading: How it functions
sections.text: Positions are auto-archived when you Close or Rebalance from the LP Pools tab. Yield entries are linked to their pool. The Loss Status flag lets you mark positions where funds were lost to hacks, mismanagement, or theft.

sections.heading: How to enter data
sections.text: Use ⚙ on any history row to edit dates, name, type, and Loss Status. To mark a loss: click ⚙, set Loss Status (Unrecoverable / Partial / Legal Action), and add a note describing what happened.

sections.heading: Further actions
sections.text: Add yield entries to closed pools via ➕ Add Yield Entry. Export to CSV for tax reporting. Update Loss Status if funds are partially recovered or legal proceedings conclude.

sections.heading: How to interpret
sections.text:
Net Result: Exit value + yield - entry value.
Beat HODL? Did pool + yield outperform simply holding the tokens?
Loss badges: ⚠ Unrecoverable · 🔶 Partial · ⚖️ Legal Action · ✅ Recovered.

sections.heading: Common questions
sections.text:
How do I record a hack or theft? Close the vault/pool at $0 exit value, then edit the history entry and set Loss Status to Unrecoverable with a note.
Can I change the loss status later? Yes — fully editable at any time via the ⚙ button.

sections.heading: What this tab does
sections.text: Watchlist for speculative tokens — tracks live price, 24h change, Vol/MC momentum, entry price, targets (T1/T2/T3), stop loss, and buy triggers. Sends Pushover alerts when prices hit your levels.

sections.heading: How it functions
sections.text: Prices fetch from CoinGecko. Vol/MC (Volume/Market Cap ratio) is tracked over 1hr, 24hr, and 48hr windows to detect momentum shifts before price moves. Sparklines show 7-day price history at a glance.

sections.heading: How to enter data
sections.text: Click + Add GEM and search for the token. Set your entry price, qty held, and targets. Use the ▶ expand panel on any row to set Stop Loss, T1/T2/T3, and Add trigger inline.

sections.heading: Further actions
sections.text: Set up Pushover in Settings (⚙ icon) for phone alerts. Use ✔ Executed? to confirm TP sells and auto-update your qty. The thesis editor (📝) lets you record your investment case for each token.

sections.heading: How to interpret
sections.text:
Vol/MC columns (1h/24h/48h): % change in volume-to-market-cap ratio vs that window. Rising Vol/MC often precedes price moves.
Alert % badge: Custom mover alert threshold — fires when 24h move exceeds this %.

sections.heading: Common questions
sections.text:
Why is live price showing — ? The CoinGecko ID is wrong — edit and search again.
What's the difference between Alert In and T1? Alert In fires when price drops TO your level (buy). T1/T2/T3 fire when price rises TO your target (sell).
How do I get phone alerts? Install Pushover app, create account, enter User Key and App Token in Settings.

faq.a: All data lives in your browser's localStorage and in a linked backup .json file on your local drive. Nothing is sent to any server. You own your data completely.

faq.q: What happens if I clear my browser cache?
faq.a: localStorage is wiped on a cache clear. If you have a linked backup file, re-open the tracker and link the file again — your data restores automatically. This is why regular Export Backup is important.

faq.q: How do I set up auto-save?
faq.a: Click the ⛁ icon → Link / Change File → select or create a .json file on your drive. Auto-save then runs every 7 minutes while the tracker is open.

CoinGecko has rate limits on the free tier. Wait 60 seconds and click ↻ Refresh. If a specific token shows — permanently, the CoinGecko ID is likely wrong — edit the entry and search for the correct ID.

faq.q: Can I use this on mobile?
faq.a: Yes — the tracker is installable as a PWA (Progressive Web App) on Android and iOS. On mobile Chrome/Safari, use the browser menu → 'Add to Home Screen'. File System auto-save works on Android Chrome; on iOS use Export Backup manually.

faq.q: How do I back up my data?
faq.a: Use ⛁ Data → Save / Export Backup to download a dated .json file. Store it somewhere safe. To restore, open the tracker and use ⛁ Data → Import / Link Backup and select your file.

IL is the difference in value between holding tokens in a liquidity pool vs simply holding them in your wallet. When token prices diverge from your entry ratio, the pool rebalances automatically and you end up with less of the outperforming token. The IL calculator on the Summary tab lets you model this for any scenario.

faq.q: What is the hedge calculator for?
faq.a: If you hold LP positions in volatile assets (BTC, ETH, SOL), a short position on a perp exchange can offset downside price risk. The Hedge tab calculates the optimal short size based on your pool exposure, current implied volatility, and funding rate.
